steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The blank separates “which ceased to be part of the empire in [year],” a dependent relative clause, from “the latter's connection to the empire is so attenuated that Spanish is seldom spoken there today,” an independent clause expressing a consequence or elaboration.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
A colon introduces an elaboration, consequence, or explanation of a preceding statement.

Option C (1648:) correctly uses the colon to signal how the Netherlands' departure in 1648 impacts its connection to Spanish.

The options with just a comma, no punctuation, or “and” all fail to properly separate these related clauses.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
